summary: Test suite covers core requirements (JUnit/JSON parsing, aggregation, flaky detection, markdown generation, workflow structure) but has critical design flaws. Aggregator.Tests.ps1 references fixtures (fixtures/run1/junit-suite-a.xml, fixtures/run1/results.json) that are never created, making unit tests non-executable as written. Inconsistent fixture approach: Workflow.Tests.ps1 creates fixtures dynamically via Invoke-ActCase, but Aggregator.Tests.ps1 expects pre-existing files without setup code. Edge case coverage is sparse (no malformed XML, encoding issues, special characters in names, duration boundary conditions). Rigor limited to happy path + basic error cases. Strengths: clear test names, good helper functions, proper cleanup, end-to-end workflow validation. The assertion that 'all tests must pass' cannot be met without completing the fixture setup in Aggregator.Tests.ps1.
